new_classes.label_en: Undenatured ethyl alcohol < 80% vol; spirits, liqueurs and other spirituous beverages (heading 2208)
new_classes.label_de: Ethylalkohol < 80 % vol, unvergällt; Branntwein, Likör und andere alkoholhaltige Getränke (Position 2208)
new_classes.definition_en: Undenatured ethyl alcohol of an alcoholic strength by volume of less than 80% vol; spirits, liqueurs and other spirituous beverages prepared either directly by distillation (with or without added flavouring) of fermented materials, or by maceration/infusion of plant materials in ethyl alcohol or distillates, generally intended for human consumption.
A production process in which a fermented substrate is distilled, with or without added flavouring, to yield a spirituous beverage falling under heading 2208.

coverage_explanation: The core distinguishing criteria are captured: (1) the alcoholic strength by volume constraint (< 80% vol) is expressed via a decimalRange restriction on alcoholByVolumePercent; (2) the undenatured character is expressed via a complement restriction distinguishing from the 2207 denatured class; (3) the production by distillation is modelled via a someValuesFrom restriction on the new DistillationProcess2208 class. However, the legal text also mentions preparation by maceration/infusion of plant materials in ethyl alcohol or distillates as an alternative production route — this second production pathway is not modelled due to the truncated legal text provided (text ends with "entweder unmittelbar durch Des[tillation]"). Additionally, the specific sub-categories (spirits, liqueurs, other spirituous beverages) are not further differentiated as they belong to subheadings rather than the heading itself. Coverage is partial due to the incomplete legal note provided and the missing maceration/infusion alternative production pathway.
